title: aconitum uncinatum subsp. noveboracense
intro: Aconitum uncinatum subsp. noveboracense, also known as eastern monk's hood or New York monk's hood, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Ranunculaceae, native to eastern North America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aconitum uncinatum subsp. noveboracense include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and wound healing. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction for medicinal purposes, although it's essential to exercise caution and consult with a medical professional before using it due to its potential toxicity.
